title: padus cornuta
intro: Padus cornuta, also known as the dogwood tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Eastern Asia, characterized by its showy white or pink flowers and red or purple berries. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Padus cornuta include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting immune function. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and can also be used in powder form to be added to food or supplements.
